user_query:
I want to email our senior software architect to ask for guidance on the most impactful topics I’ve been exploring. Please use the local inputs to prioritize and then draft a concise, enthusiastic email.

Inputs:
- data/topics.csv (canonical topic records)
- config/weights.yaml (weights, filter, and top_n)
- All Markdown files under data/notes/ (supporting context and questions keyed by TopicID)

Requirements:
1) Inspect the directory data/notes/ and read every .md file. For each topic in data/topics.csv, locate matching sections by the exact token "TopicID: <ID>" (e.g., "TopicID: T3"). From those sections, extract:
   - The single line beginning with "Question:" (use this as the question to include in the email)
   - The single line beginning with "Context:" (use one sentence of this as brief context in the email)
   If a topic appears in more than one note file, keep the first occurrence from each file and include all file paths in a deduplicated list.

2) Read config/weights.yaml to determine:
   - weights.impact and weights.urgency for computing a composite score: composite_score = (impact * weights.impact) + (urgency * weights.urgency)
   - filter.field and filter.value to filter rows in data/topics.csv (only topics whose filter field equals the given value)
   - top_n (the number of topics to include in the email)

3) Filter, sort, and rank records: from data/topics.csv, apply the filter from step 2, compute composite_score for each remaining topic, and sort descending by composite_score. Break ties deterministically by: higher impact, then higher urgency, then lexicographical topic_id.

Deliverables:
- output/topic_rankings.csv with columns exactly: topic_id,topic,urgency,impact,composite_score,source_note_files
  - source_note_files must be a "|"-delimited list of the distinct note file paths in which the topic’s TopicID was found.
- output/email_draft_to_architect.md containing a ready-to-send draft that includes:
  - A first line Subject: that contains the phrase "prioritized architecture topics"
  - A concise greeting and one-sentence intro stating that you prioritized topics by impact and urgency using the weights from config/weights.yaml
  - A ranked list of the top_n topics in order (1..top_n). For each item, include: topic_id, topic title, the extracted Question line (verbatim after the colon), and one brief context sentence derived from the extracted Context line.
  - A polite ask for a 30-minute chat and an invitation for any recommended resources.

Use only the provided local files. Save outputs to the specified paths. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
